Address
215 FIRST STREET N E, SPRINGHILL, LA 71075

Setting
Rural
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.22 4.11 3.03 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.28 0.72 0.26 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.22 0.91 1.15 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.72 2.48 1.62 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.50 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.05 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.71
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.19
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.55
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
New facility — insufficient data.
RN turnover
Staffing penalized (missing PBJ submission).
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.46
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.06
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-01-15 5 5 0 28 1 28
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-12-06 5 4 1 36 1 36
Total weighted health score
30.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (15)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

13 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-19
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2025-01-15 2025-02-19
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-19
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-19
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. E 2025-01-15 2025-02-19
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. E 2023-12-06 2024-01-19
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted E 2023-12-06 2024-01-19
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2023-12-06 2024-01-19
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2023-12-06 2024-01-19
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2023-09-20 2023-09-15
0606 Not hire anyone with a finding of abuse, neglect, exploitation, or theft. E 2022-12-08 2023-01-05
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2022-12-08 2023-01-05
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. K 2022-12-08 2023-01-05
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2022-12-08 2023-01-05
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. K 2022-12-08 2023-01-05
